title: Dead Lettering, Poison Messages, and Parking · Runtime Contract: Quorum Delivery Limit
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must interpret x-death evidence defensively, prevent cycles, retain bounded context, and authorize replay through a separate path; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the quorum delivery limit increment in a Go poison-message remediation workflow. Learners progre…
objectives: Account for RabbitMQ 4.x quorum delivery limit defaults, x-delivery-count, policy changes, group requeues, and intentional dead-letter retention; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
